mechanism_narrative: A persistent decline in the federal funds rate reduces the risk-free discount rate, directly boosting equity valuations and yielding positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate falls below 5.0 percent, labor market tightness accelerates wage growth and consumer spending, driving corporate earnings revisions upward and producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 5-day forward returns in more than 55% of instances where UNRATE drops below 5.0%, or the signal's directional accuracy drops below 52% over a 3-year out-of-sample test.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous acceleration in industrial production and contraction in consumer credit availability signals a supply-demand mismatch that compresses corporate margins, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict negative 60-day returns in more than 45% of triggered periods, or the combined Rank IC of INDPRO and RSAFS exceeds 0.05 in the positive direction.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of rapid policy tightening, rising short-term rates amplify the drag on interest-sensitive housing demand, causing a sharp decline in construction starts that transmits to broader equity valuations and yields negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS rises > 0.25 percentage points in a single month
failure_condition: Forward 20-day returns are positive in more than 60% of months where FEDFUNDS jumps by 0.25pp or more, or the signal shows no statistical edge over a buy-and-hold benchmark in a 5-year walk-forward test.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained month-over-month increase in total nonfarm payrolls reflects robust aggregate demand and business expansion, which directly supports equity earnings growth and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The signal produces negative 5-day forward returns in more than 55% of positive payroll growth months, or the rolling 1-year directional accuracy falls below 50%.

mechanism_narrative: When real GDP growth exceeds 3.5 percent annualized, the economy enters an overheating phase that triggers inflationary pressures and forces the Federal Reserve to adopt a hawkish stance, ultimately depressing equity valuations and yielding negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 annualized growth rate exceeds 3.5 percent
failure_condition: SPX forward returns remain positive in more than 60% of quarters where GDPC1 growth surpasses 3.5%, or the signal's predictive power vanishes after controlling for CPIAUCSL inflation trends.

mechanism_narrative: The combination of rising federal funds rates and a contracting unemployment rate signals a labor market that is outpacing monetary policy control, creating earnings volatility and risk-off positioning that drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint FEDFUNDS-UNRATE signal fails to predict negative 20-day returns in more than 50% of observed periods, or the interaction term in a multivariate regression shows a positive coefficient with p > 0.10.

mechanism_narrative: In a post-recession recovery phase, a sharp rebound in industrial production coupled with stable consumer credit availability indicates strong capacity utilization without overheating, which supports corporate profit margins and produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward 60-day returns are negative in more than 55% of post-recovery periods where INDPRO rises and RSAFS stabilizes, or the signal's outperformance over the S&P 500 benchmark drops below 100 basis points over a 3-year window.
